name: Dade's Theorem

phase_0.boundary_condition: The universal property of the quotient lattice — all homomorphisms factor uniquely through it

VINIT.justification: Before Dade's theorem applies, we have a raw lattice with no equivalence imposed — the void state before structure is recognized

TANCH.justification: The terminal anchor that contains and closes the entire system — the final structure after congruence is applied

AFWD.chosen_element: the canonical projection π: L → L/θ
AFWD.justification: Forward morphism — the directed transformation from the original lattice toward the quotient structure

AREV.chosen_element: the lifting of homomorphisms back to L
AREV.justification: Reverse morphism — descent from the quotient back to the original, recovering pre-image structure

CLINK.justification: Sequential chaining — Dade's theorem chains multiple homomorphisms and congruences into a single coherent structure

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: the identity homomorphism id: L → L
IMSCRIB.justification: Self-reference — the lattice recognizes itself as the fixed point of the congruence relation

FSPLIT.chosen_element: the congruence relation θ partitions L into equivalence classes
FSPLIT.justification: Co-multiplication — one lattice L branches into two distinct aspects: the partition {[a]_θ} and the quotient structure L/θ

FFUSE.chosen_element: the reconstruction of L from its quotient via the universal property
FFUSE.justification: Multiplication — the equivalence classes and quotient structure reconstitute the original lattice L exactly; FFUSE(FSPLIT(L)) = L

EVALT.chosen_element: the homomorphism φ: L → M satisfies the congruence condition
EVALT.justification: Affirmative branch — when φ respects θ (i.e., a ≡ b (mod θ) ⟹ φ(a) = φ(b)), the factorization succeeds

EVALF.chosen_element: the homomorphism φ fails to respect the congruence
EVALF.justification: Negative branch — when φ does not preserve the equivalence relation, no unique factorization exists

ENGAGR.chosen_element: a homomorphism that is both congruence-respecting and congruence-violating in different components
ENGAGR.justification: Paradice — Dade's theorem permits partial homomorphisms that hold both success and failure simultaneously in a bicameral structure

IFIX.chosen_element: the permanent record of the factorization φ̄: L/θ → M
IFIX.justification: Irreversible fixation — once the unique factorization is established, it becomes an append-only fact in the lattice's history

phase_2.fuse_result: original lattice L (recovered from partition and quotient structure)

phase_3.void_description: 00 — the unquotiented lattice L before any congruence is imposed; no equivalence structure exists
phase_3.true_description: 01 — the homomorphism φ respects the congruence θ; factorization φ̄ exists and is unique
phase_3.false_description: 10 — the homomorphism φ violates the congruence; no valid factorization can be constructed
phase_3.both_description: 11 — a bicameral state where the homomorphism simultaneously respects and violates the congruence in different lattice components (paradice)

steps.domain_action: unquotiented lattice L — the void state before congruence is recognized
canonical projection π: L → L/θ — forward morphism toward quotient structure
congruence relation θ partitions L into equivalence classes [a]_θ and quotient L/θ — one lattice branches into two aspects
homomorphism φ: L → M respects congruence (a ≡ b (mod θ) ⟹ φ(a) = φ(b)) — affirmative branch
the induced homomorphism φ̄: L/θ → M is well-defined — forward morphism on the T-arm
φ̄ ∘ π = φ — self-reference: the factorization recognizes itself as the unique solution
reconstruction of L from equivalence classes and quotient — branches reconstitute the original lattice
homomorphism φ fails to respect congruence — negative branch
attempt to lift φ back to L fails — reverse morphism cannot recover a valid factorization
no valid reconstruction on the F-arm — the failure state is sealed
composition of multiple homomorphisms φ₁ ∘ φ₂ ∘ ... ∘ φₙ — sequential chaining of congruence-respecting maps
the chain recognizes itself as a single coherent morphism — identity through composition
a homomorphism that is both congruence-respecting and congruence-violating in different lattice components — paradice held without resolution
composition with the paradice element — the chain continues through both states simultaneously
the factorization φ̄: L/θ → M is permanently recorded — append-only fixation of the unique solution
the quotient lattice L/θ with all factorizations established — terminal anchor that closes the system
the entire structure recognizes itself as a self-referential closed loop — Dade's theorem as identity

phase_5.compiler_frontend: the congruence relation θ — it translates the intention to quotient L into a partition and equivalence structure
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: homomorphism preservation — components communicate by respecting the congruence relation; φ(a ≡ b) ⟹ φ(a) = φ(b)
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the equivalence classes [a]_θ and the quotient lattice L/θ — state is stored as partitions and their induced structure
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the universal property — it determines the unique order and timing of factorization: first partition, then induce, then verify
phase_5.alfs_store: the canonical projection π: L → L/θ — the bootstrap store and reference for all subsequent homomorphisms

phase_6.cycle_cost: the cardinality of the congruence relation |θ| — each equivalence class costs one unit of structural information
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: lattice L with full granularity — all elements distinct, no equivalence imposed
phase_6.post_cycle_state: quotient lattice L/θ with coarser granularity — equivalent elements merged, structure preserved
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — Dade's theorem is entropy-conservative because the universal property ensures no information is lost; the quotient structure is a lossless compression of L under the congruence relation

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → AFWD → FSPLIT → EVALT → AFWD → IMSCRIB → FFUSE → EVALF → AREV → FFUSE → CLINK → IMSCRIB → ENGAGR → CLINK → IFIX → TANCH → IMSCRIB
-- Class: Dade's Theorem
-- Fingerprint: sig=(10,3,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=17
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(2, 6)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑾  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑼 → 𐑚  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [2] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [3] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [4] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑚 → 𐑙  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [5] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑚 → 𐑙  | identity — self-imscription
--   [6] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑖  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [7] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑙 → 𐑗  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [8] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑖 → 𐑙  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [9] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑗 → 𐑱  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [10] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑙 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [11] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑳  | identity — self-imscription
--   [12] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑠 → 𐑱  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [13] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑳 → 𐑭  | composition — regime coherence
--   [14] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑱 → 𐑡  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [15] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑭 → 𐑠  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [16] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑡 → 𐑼  | identity — self-imscription

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def dades_theorem_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑠 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑾)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (unquotiented lattice L — the void state before congruence is recognized)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑼 𐑚)  -- [1] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (canonical projection π: L → L/θ — forward morphism toward quotient structure)
  -- FSPLIT [2] (gran := 𐑚) (congruence relation θ partitions L into equivalence classes [a]_θ and quotient L/θ — one lattice branches into two aspects) / FFUSE [6] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (3 nodes)
      .seq
        (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [3] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (homomorphism φ: L → M respects congruence (a ≡ b (mod θ) ⟹ φ(a) = φ(b)) — aff...)
      .seq
        (.arrow 𐑾 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [4] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (the induced homomorphism φ̄: L/θ → M is well-defined — forward morphism on th...)
        (.arrow 𐑠 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [5] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (φ̄ ∘ π = φ — self-reference: the factorization recognizes itself as the uniqu...)
      -- F-branch (0 nodes)
      (.refl 𐑙))  -- F-branch: empty arc (direct to FFUSE.F)
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [6]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑖)  -- [6] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (reconstruction of L from equivalence classes and quotient — branches reconstitute the original lattice)
  (.arrow 𐑖 𐑙 𐑗)  -- [7] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (homomorphism φ fails to respect congruence — negative branch)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑖 𐑙)  -- [8] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (attempt to lift φ back to L fails — reverse morphism cannot recover a valid f...)
  (.arrow 𐑙 𐑗 𐑱)  -- [9] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 | fuse μ — assembly mode (no valid reconstruction on the F-arm — the failure state is sealed)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑙 𐑠)  -- [10] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (composition of multiple homomorphisms φ₁ ∘ φ₂ ∘ ... ∘ φₙ — sequential chainin...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑳)  -- [11] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (the chain recognizes itself as a single coherent morphism — identity through ...)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑠 𐑱)  -- [12] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (a homomorphism that is both congruence-respecting and congruence-violating in...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑳 𐑭)  -- [13] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (composition with the paradice element — the chain continues through both stat...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑱 𐑡)  -- [14] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (the factorization φ̄: L/θ → M is permanently recorded — append-only fixation ...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑠)  -- [15] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (the quotient lattice L/θ with all factorizations established — terminal ancho...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑡 𐑼)  -- [16] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (the entire structure recognizes itself as a self-referential closed loop — Da...)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def dades_theorem_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑠 :=
  (dades_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def dades_theorem_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑠 :=
  (dades_theorem_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem dades_theorem_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
